definitions.client: SwarmJelly is the specialist AI we built to repair other AI. It's 4 billion parameters · about 1/100th the size of the big commercial models · but it's trained on 125,000 specific examples of what goes wrong with AI agents and how to fix them. You don't have to use it · the weights are open · you can host it yourself on a HoneyBox. We just ask that the receipts keep flowing through the network so the defense gets stronger for everyone.
definitions.cre_operator: In CRE every shop has a **specialist appraiser** for the asset class. The industrial broker doesn't call the residential appraiser. The cold-storage broker doesn't call the multifamily comp guy. You call the specialist · because the specialist has seen the comp set · knows the failure modes · knows what the building should pencil at. SwarmJelly is the specialist appraiser for **failed agent outputs**. It's seen 125,000 of them in training. It knows the failure shape on sight.
definitions.defendableos: SwarmJelly is a 4B-parameter language model fine-tuned in-house on a 125,000-pair corpus organized as a **7-failure-mode × 5-RJ-task taxonomy** with a 25% per-mode distribution cap. It ships as `swarmjelly-4b:v1.2` (current production tag) and runs at `whale:11434`. It is the model that backs SwarmFixer · every refinery 5-task output is a SwarmJelly inference call at TEMP=0.05. The model is open-weights · MIT-with-receipt-clause license · operators can self-host on a HoneyBox.
definitions.street: "The Jelly model." That's what the Hacks call it at standup. **SwarmJelly** is the in-house cooked 4-billion-parameter model that powers SwarmFixer. It's not the offense brain · it's not the customer's agent · it's the **defense model** that knows how to repair other models' work. Trained in-house. Open weights. Deployed at `whale:11434`. TEMP locked at 0.05.
